reasoning: The FedRAMP SC-7 (18) control focuses on ensuring boundary protection and fail-secure configurations, which in Kubernetes can be mapped to network security and ingress configurations. NetworkPolicies can enforce default deny rules for ingress and egress, ensuring that no unintended traffic flows in or out of the cluster. Additionally, ensuring that ingress resources use TLS and restricting the use of LoadBalancer services to prevent public exposure aligns with the fail-secure principle.
title: Boundary Protection | Fail Secure
